name: Ah Fatt Kelong Fishing
description: An offshore fishing platform (kelong) experience; facilities are decent for fishing enthusiasts and group trips.

name: Batu Ayam Penyabong
description: A unique rock formation at Penyabong Beach resembling a rooster, associated with local legend/folklore.

name: Bluewater Express
description: The primary ferry service provider for travelers commuting between Mersing jetty and Tioman Island.

name: Bukit Selantai
description: A hiking trail offering panoramic views of the coastline and surrounding islands.

name: Endau Rompin National Park
description: One of the world's oldest rainforests containing waterfalls (Buaya Sangkut), indigenous villages, and endangered wildlife like the Malayan Tiger.

name: Fushun Temple
description: A friendly Chinese temple located in the neighborhood, suitable for a cultural stroll.

name: Gunung Arong
description: Recreational forest offering a hike with a rewarding view of the South China Sea.

name: Kuil Sri Subramaniam
description: A brightly decorated Hindu temple located at the main roundabout, situated right next to the Chinese temple.

name: Masjid Jamek Bandar Mersing
description: Iconic hilltop mosque offering panoramic views of Mersing town and the sea.

name: Mersing Esplanade
description: A waterfront stretch lined with budget hotels and eateries; a popular social spot to meet and chat.

name: Mersing Harbour Centre
description: The main transport hub for island hopping, also features local souvenir shops.

name: Muzium Mersing
description: Local museum showcasing the district's history, Japanese occupation artifacts, and cultural heritage.

name: OrcaNation Dive Center (Rawa Island)
description: Asia’s leading PADI 5-star Instructor Development Center, focusing on diving experiences for families and children.

name: Pantai Air Papan
description: A popular picnic spot with shallow waters, lined with rice paddies and village chalets.

name: Pantai Pasir Lanun
description: "Pirate's Sand Beach," a secluded beach spot with a mythical history.

name: Pantai Penyabung
description: A quieter, scenic beach near Endau, popular for photography and relaxation.

name: Pantai Tanjung Resang
description: Located in Endau; known for clear waters, snorkeling, diving, and sunsets. Nearby lodging available at Teluk Sari Beach Resort.

name: Pantai Tg Leman
description: A coastal area serving as a gateway to offshore islands; listed as an eco-tourism site.

name: Pulau Aur
description: Far offshore island famous for crystal clear waters; a hotspot for deep-sea enthusiasts.

name: Pulau Besar
description: Former location of a reality TV show (Survivor); known for pristine waters and jungle trekking.

name: Pulau Dayang
description: Located next to Pulau Aur; a popular destination for sport fishing and diving.

name: Pulau Harimau
description: "Tiger Island," known for its unique cavern and diving spots.

name: Pulau Hujong
description: One of the outer islands in the Mersing Marine Park, noted for eco-tourism.

name: Pulau Mawar
description: A small tidal island accessible by foot during low tide; famous for the "Heaven's Gate" rock arch.

name: Pulau Mensirip
description: A small, scenic island often visited for snorkeling stops.

name: Pulau Mentigi
description: A smaller, quieter island located near the larger Pulau Tinggi.

name: Pulau Pemanggil
description: Famous for deep-sea fishing and hiking routes to view the South China Sea.

name: Pulau Sibu
description: Situated in Eastern Johor; features white sandy beaches and diverse coral reefs. Popular for honeymoons and scuba diving.

name: Pulau Tengah
description: A marine park island known for conservation efforts and clear waters.

name: Pulau Tinggi (General’s Hat Island)
description: Secluded island rising 2000ft above sea level with a traditional Malay settlement. Surrounded by 8 smaller islets; ideal for relaxing.

name: Rawa Island (Pulau Rawa)
description: Known as the "Malaysian Maldives" for its white sands and iconic water slide.

name: Setindan View
description: A scenic viewing point listed as a local eco-tourism destination.

name: Tasik Hijau Jemaluang
description: An "Emerald Lake" (Tasik Hijau) likely formed from mining remnants, offering unique photo opportunities.

name: Tioman Island (Pulau Tioman)
description: Famous ecological paradise with duty-free status, diving spots, and rainforests.

name: VReal Mersing Island Hopping
description: Provides "Virtual and Real" island hopping packages to explore the pristine beaches and scenery of Mersing's islands.
